title: K-200 GCS Software Architecture Document v1.0
text:
# K-200 GCS Software Architecture Document

Document number: MD-DOC-479-A. Revision: A. Type: specification. Author: Jordan Kim. Created: 2024-11-15. Last modified: 2024-11-15. Status: Released.

Background: The GCS architecture was refactored from a monolithic design (SRC-001 gcs_main.py, 8,400 lines) to the v2 backend/frontend split documented here. Change authority: ECN-320 / ECR-225. Design review: WIKI-058 (GCS Architecture Review, 2024-10-15).

## §2 Architecture Overview

**Backend service (SRC-024 gcs_ui_backend.py):** asyncio Python process. Owns all drone communications via P-1167 (GCS Radio Unit). Receives and decodes MAVLink telemetry from the drone via P-1126 (radio) and P-1127 (encryption/decryption FPGA). Encodes and sends command packets. Maintains drone state machine. Exposes a WebSocket API on localhost:8765 for the frontend.

**Frontend:** Vue.js SPA served locally by SRC-024. Displays real-time telemetry, map (SRC-025 replaced by Leaflet JS), camera feed, sonar overlay, and mission planning tools (SRC-017 mission_planner.py adapted to SRC-024 WebSocket API).

**Encryption integration:** SRC-024 calls SRC-018 (encryption_handler.c, Lattice iCE40 AES-256-GCM FPGA driver) via Python ctypes. Session key management handled in SRC-024.

## §3 Archived Components

SRC-001 (gcs_main.py v1.8.4): archived, not maintained. SRC-002 (gcs_telemetry.py): functionality absorbed into SRC-024. SRC-025 (gcs_map_layer.py): replaced by Leaflet JS map component.

## §4 Interfaces

GCS Radio Unit (P-1167) → SRC-024 (serial/UDP) → WebSocket → Frontend. Encryption: P-1127 FPGA module inline between P-1121 (datalink controller) and P-1167. Protocol: DOC-471 (Comms ICD Rev C), DOC-472 (Protocol Spec Rev B).
